title: tetraglochin alata
intro: Tetraglochin alata, also known as the tree daisy, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Tetraglochin alata are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to promote digestive health and boost the immune system.
